system.name: Urinary System
system.shortDescription: Kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra. Filters blood (~180L/day), maintains fluid/electrolyte/acid-base balance, regulates blood pressure (RAAS), produces erythropoietin and activates vitamin D.

primaryStructures: Nephrons (~1 million per kidney) — glomerulus, Bowman's capsule, proximal tubule, loop of Henle, distal tubule, collecting duct
Urethra (longer in male, shorter in female)

voiding_path: Calyces → renal pelvis → ureter → bladder → urethra

anatomy.vertebralCorrelates: Per Cornell: T11-L2 dorsal-lumbar relates to kidneys, ureters. L1-L2 → bladder. Bodyworkers: lumbar mobilization + psoas (which overlies kidneys) supports urinary function.
anatomy.nervePlexus: Renal plexus (sympathetic from celiac + aorticorenal). Hypogastric + vesical plexus (bladder). Pudendal nerve (external sphincter).

astrologicalRulership.primaryPlanetRationale: Venus rules Libra, which rules the kidneys per Cornell. Cornell (1933): 'Venus rules the kidneys, the lumbar region.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Kidneys (primary), renal function, balance/filtration as Venusian harmony
Bladder inflammation, UTI, hematuria, kidney stones (sharp Mars)
Bladder, fluid output, urinary fluid balance
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Moon rules all fluids including urinary.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Chronic renal disease, kidney stones (crystalline Saturn), urinary retention
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Cornell: 'Saturn in Libra → chronic renal weakness.'

secondaryPlanets.rules: Bladder (Scorpio rules bladder), deep eliminative function, prostate-bladder interface
secondaryPlanets.rationale: Pluto modern ruler of Scorpio = bladder/elimination

secondaryPlanets.rules: Idiopathic urinary symptoms, interstitial cystitis, urinary-emotional connection

axes.name: Renal Axis
axes.interpretation: THE primary axis for this system. Aries (adrenals — sit ON the kidneys anatomically) opposite Libra (kidneys proper). Adrenal-renal interface critical for blood pressure, fluid balance, stress-urinary connection.

indicatorPattern.aries_dominant: High BP, adrenal overdrive, sympathetic urinary irritability
indicatorPattern.libra_dominant: Low BP, renal weakness, fluid retention
indicatorPattern.balanced: Smooth renal function, balanced BP, healthy fluid distribution

axes.name: Regenerative Axis (urinary-reproductive overlay)
axes.interpretation: Scorpio rules bladder + reproductive — close anatomical overlap. UTI vs vaginitis differential common. Pelvic floor governs both systems.

energetics.chakra: Sacral (Svadhisthana) for bladder/lower; Heart's lower border for kidneys (some traditions)
energetics.nervousSystemPathway: Renal sympathetic = vasoconstriction; parasympathetic via pelvic nerves for voiding
energetics.meridianCrossover_TCM: Kidney meridian (CRITICAL in TCM — governs essence/jing, bones, will, fear). Bladder meridian (longest meridian, runs along entire back).

cellSalts.indicatedFor: Acidic urine, uric acid crystals, gout-tendency. Carey's PRIMARY salt for this system.
Excess water, gallbladder-kidney axis, biliousness affecting urinary.

supplementaryHerbs.name: Cranberry
supplementaryHerbs.mechanism: Proanthocyanidins prevent E. coli adhesion to bladder wall
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: UTI prevention (NOT acute treatment — too late at infection)

supplementaryHerbs.name: D-Mannose
supplementaryHerbs.mechanism: Sugar that E. coli binds to instead of bladder wall
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: UTI prevention + early treatment of E. coli UTI

supplementaryHerbs.name: Uva ursi (bearberry)
supplementaryHerbs.mechanism: Arbutin → hydroquinone antimicrobial in urine (works in alkaline urine — take with baking soda)
supplementaryHerbs.indicatedFor: Acute UTI — short course only (toxicity if prolonged)

scents.name: Juniper (caution)
scents.mechanism: Traditional urinary support — AVOID with active kidney disease

modalityRecommendations.individual: Hydration is #1 (2L+ water/day for most adults). Cranberry/D-mannose for UTI prevention (especially if recurrent). Address pelvic floor (Kegels for both incontinence AND overactive bladder — opposite presentations, same muscles). UTI symptoms (burning, urgency, frequency) → MD if not resolved with home care in 24-48hr.
modalityRecommendations.medical_astrologer: Venus + Libra primary. Aries-Libra renal axis. Mars in urinary signs for acute UTI. Saturn for chronic renal. Pluto for bladder/deep elimination. Outer planet transits to natal Venus or Libra significant.
modalityRecommendations.reiki: Sacral chakra extended. Orange light visualization. Hand position over lower abdomen (bladder) and lower back (kidneys).
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_general: Psoas release (kidneys sit anteriorly on psoas). QL release. Lumbar mobilization. Pelvic floor (specialized training). Visceral kidney techniques (advanced).
modalityRecommendations.bodywork_contraindications: Active acute UTI: defer (fever risk). Known kidney disease: caution with deep work. Recent surgery, pregnancy: precautions.
modalityRecommendations.naturopathy: Hydration, cranberry/D-mannose prevention, uva ursi acute (short course), marshmallow for IC, dandelion/nettle for fluid balance. Address recurrent UTI patterns (post-coital, post-menopausal). Refer for nephrology if elevated creatinine/BUN.
modalityRecommendations.acupuncture: Kidney meridian (KD 3 Taixi master point). Bladder meridian (UB 23 Shenshu over kidneys). Ren Mai for bladder.
modalityRecommendations.ayurveda_phase2_placeholder: Mutravaha Srotas (urinary channels). Apana Vata (downward elimination). Phase 2.
modalityRecommendations.pastoral_spiritual: Kidneys hold fear (TCM). Release work for stored fear. 'I release what no longer serves.'

pathologies.name: UTI (Urinary Tract Infection)
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Mars in Libra/Scorpio → urinary inflammation, cystitis.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: UTI symptoms (burning urination, urgency, frequency, suprapubic pain) — early intervention with D-mannose, cranberry, increased fluids may help. If symptoms persist > 24-48 hours, fever develops, back pain occurs, or blood appears in urine — see your healthcare provider for evaluation and likely antibiotics.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Mars-urinary sign configurations classically associated with UTI patterns. Most uncomplicated UTI in healthy adult woman responds to short antibiotic course. Refer for: fever, back pain (rule out pyelonephritis), pregnancy, immunocompromise, men, children, recurrent. Holistic prevention strategies appropriate after acute resolution.

modalityNotes.bodywork: Defer during acute. Recovery: pelvic floor work helpful.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Hydration, D-mannose, cranberry, urinating after intercourse, front-to-back wiping, cotton underwear

redFlags: UTI in pregnancy → MD same day

pathologies.name: Kidney Stones (Nephrolithiasis)
pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Renal calculi — Saturn in Libra; mineral deposition under restricted flow.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Kidney stones produce some of the most severe pain in medicine (renal colic). Suspected kidney stone → urgent medical evaluation. Prevention (especially if recurrent): hydration (2-3L/day), reduce oxalate-rich foods if calcium oxalate type, address dietary triggers.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn in Libra classically associated with renal calculi (Cornell, 1933). Acute stones are medical (CT imaging, pain control, possible urology intervention). Prevention: 24-hour urine collection identifies stone type → tailored intervention (low oxalate for Ca-oxalate, low purine for uric acid, etc.). Refer to urology/nephrology.

modalityNotes.bodywork: Active colic = ED. Prevention phase: lumbar work, lymphatic drainage.
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Hydration MOST important. Dietary modifications per stone type.

pathologies.name: Urinary Incontinence

planetaryCombinations: Saturn in Libra (weakened pelvic floor with age)
transitTriggers: Outer planet transits with age-related onset

pathologies.classicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn afflictions in urinary signs → loss of control function.'

indicatorLanguage.individual: Urinary incontinence is COMMON and TREATABLE — please don't suffer in silence. Pelvic floor physical therapy is highly effective for most types. Specialized care available.
indicatorLanguage.practitioner: Saturn-urinary configurations align with sphincter weakness patterns. Differentiate: stress (effort/cough/sneeze), urge (overactive bladder), mixed, overflow. Pelvic floor PT first-line. Refer to urogynecology / urology for severe or refractory.

modalityNotes.bodywork: Pelvic floor specialized training. Sacral work. AVOID kegels if pelvic floor is HYPERtonic (common mistake — assess first).
modalityNotes.lifestyle: Pelvic floor PT, bladder retraining, weight management, address constipation, avoid irritants (caffeine, alcohol, artificial sweeteners)

redFlags: Sudden onset with neurological symptoms → urgent (rule out spinal cord)

bodywork.contraindicationsCriticallity: MODERATE — acute UTI, recent kidney surgery

acupuncturist_tcm.outputDepth: Major TCM emphasis on Kidney essence

complianceNotes.scopeOfPractice: Acute renal failure, suspected stones, hematuria, complicated UTI all medical. Holistic adjunctive.
criticalReferralRules: Severe flank pain + nausea → ED (kidney stone)
Suspected acute kidney injury → urgent MD
UTI in pregnancy → MD same day
